name: Session 2026-04-07

messages.content:

The two expressions you pointed out exceed the separator-width heuristic once indentation is counted, so this needs to be an explicit style rule rather than something derivable from the current width guidance. I’m reading the surrounding standard text before I patch it so the new rule doesn’t contradict the existing split-call and standalone-ternary rules.

The current standard only talks about standalone multiline ternaries and ternaries inside parenthesized calls. It doesn’t cover direct assignment style at all, so I’m checking existing code to phrase the new rule in a way that matches the codebase instead of inventing a one-off exception.
